hypothesis: If a single classifier-free guided score-based diffusion model is trained only on metastable-state MD data for states A and B and its reverse SDE is steered by score-based interpolation and then refined by score-aligned ascent using r_SAA = s^B − s^A, then it will sample transition-state structures with near-0.5 committors and accurate saddle-point geometries that outperform linear/geodesic interpolation and either component alone, because the difference of conditional scores approximates the reaction-coordinate direction and the ascent projects energy increases along this direction while suppressing orthogonal modes.
